=PP r
CRAIG S.J. JOHNS 
i.,
1999 Ilsripn Street
Oakland, CA 94612
610) 76&2mO
September 18, 1992
Mr. John lVallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" Sheet, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95812
Re: 
Request for Formal Advice Regzriling Potential
Conflict of Interests
Dear Mr. Wallace:
I am writing to request formal advice pursuant to California Government Code
Section 83114(b) and 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18329G) regarding my
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (CaI. Gov't.
Code 0 81000 et. seo. arld 2 CaI. Code Regs. $ 18700 et. seo..). I am a Member ofthe
California Regional Water Quality Control Board, San FYancisco Bay Region ("Regional
Board"). This letter contains all facts material to rny inquiry.
The circumstances underlying this request are as follows. I am an Associate with
the Oaklancl law firm of Crosby, Heafey, Roach & May Professional Corporation
("Crosby"). I am a salaried employee of Crosby and enjoy no fmancial or other ownership
interest in the frrm. The firm represents a number of clients in a variety of legal
matters, Some of these clients occasionally have, or are int€rested in, matters which are
before the Regional Board and require some action on the part of tJ:e Regional Board,
incluiling, for example, permit issuance, development and adoption of the Regional Water
QuaIiW Basin PIan, or enforcement of applicable $,at€r quality statutes and regulations.
On the agenda before the Regional Board for the month of October 1992 is a
Tentative Order that would amend certain Waste Discharge Requirements (the "Tentative
Order") for SheII Oil Company ("SheII"), Martinez Manufacturing Complex. I have
enclosed, for your information, a tlraft copy of the Tentative Order that will be presenteil
to the Regional Board in October.
Crosby represents SheII in matters regarding tort liabitity issues related to SheII's
facility in Martinez. Crosby does not represent SheII in any matters related to the


Mr. John WaIIace
Fair Political hactices Commission
September 18, 1992
Page 2
Tentative Order before the Regional Board in October, 1992. I receive no reportable
income or gi-fts from SheII, am involved in no contractual matters with Shell, and I own
no stock or other securities in Shell.
Under these circumstances, is a conllict of interest deemed to exist which would
require me to abstain from participating in, voting on, or attempting to influence a
decision concerning the Shell matter pending before the Regional Board? I am
particularly interest€d in your opinion regarding the question presenteal in light of .Iz re
Thorner, 1 FPPC Ops. 198 (1970, and recent informal advice letters the FPPC has issued
in In re Svbert No. I-91-29D and In re: Winston No. I-90-230).
Moreover, I am interested in your opinion concerning an area not specifically
addressed in Svbert and -UIzl&4 related to "foreseeability' of potential effects on indirect
economic interests. Using the SheII matter as an example, would the revenue generated
by Crosby's representation of Shell in the tort liability matt€rs be taken into account in
assessing the'foreseeability" issue?
The Shell matter is scheduled to be heard by t,Le Regional Boaril on October 21,
1992. Therefore, I would greatly appreciat€ your guidance as soon as possible. I
appreciate your attention to and assistance with tJ:is matter. kior to issuing your formal
advice letter, I would appreciate the opportunity to speak with you if you have any
questions concerning the factual background or other elements of this request.
CSIJ:bs
Enclosure
cc: Gary Grimm, Esq.
William Atwater, Esq.
Steve Ritchie
